How to request and process account locks, and the criteria for unlocking.

When to lock

Lock an account when there's an active dispute, a confirmed compliance flag, or a request from legal.

Unlock criteria

  • Dispute resolved with documentation.
  • Compliance review cleared by manager.
  • Legal hold released in writing.

When to lock

Lock an account when there's an active dispute, a confirmed compliance flag, or a request from legal.

Unlock criteria

  • Compliance review cleared by manager.
  • Legal hold released in writing.
Triggers for automated daily debit suspension and the approval path.

Triggers

  • Two consecutive NSF returns.
  • Merchant request with hardship documentation.
  • Disputed balance pending review.

Approval

ADDS requires supervisor approval. The suspension lasts up to 7 calendar days unless extended in writing.

Triggers

Pre-approved ticket templates for the most common admin support requests.

Templates

  • Balance correction — include account ID, expected balance, supporting docs.
  • ACH change — include voided check or bank letter.
  • Contact update — include source of new contact and verification.
A call missing the required disclosure — compliance risk and remediation.

What went wrong

The agent skipped the recording disclosure on an outbound call. The call was flagged in QA review.

Required

The recording disclosure is the first thing you say after the merchant identifies themselves. Always.

How to set up a new payment arrangement on a charged-off account.

What qualifies as a new PA

A new payment arrangement (New PA) is created the first time a charged-off account agrees to a structured repayment plan after charge-off.

Required steps

  • Verify identity and right party contact.
  • Confirm balance and terms in writing.
  • Set the first debit date no more than 14 days out.
  • Document the PA in the account note with effective date.

Setting expectations

"This arrangement keeps your account out of further legal review as long as the agreed payments come through. Missed payments revert the account to active charge-off status."

A call where the agent dominated the line — what to fix and why.

What went wrong

  • Agent interrupted three times in the first 60 seconds.
  • Pitched a plan before learning the merchant's situation.
  • Closed the call without confirmation in writing.

Coaching note

Slow down. Discovery is not a step you can skip — it's the step that makes the close possible.

  • Agent interrupted three times in the first 60 seconds.

A model Rehab 2 call — strong open, real discovery, two-option close.

Why this is a good call

  • Used the merchant's name three times in the first 30 seconds.
  • Asked open-ended discovery before offering options.
  • Closed with a two-option choice instead of an open prompt.

Listen for

Pacing slows in the middle so the merchant can respond. The agent doesn't fill silence with new offers — they let the merchant decide.

Final paperwork and notation steps once a settlement offer is accepted.

What gets sent

  • Settlement letter with effective date and final amount.
  • Receipt of payment confirmation.
  • Account status note: "Settled — closed."
Servicing a payment arrangement that's already in flight on a charged-off account.

Daily handling

An Active PA means the account already has a working repayment schedule. Your job is to keep it in flight.

What to watch

  • Missed debit attempt — outreach within 24 hours.
  • Bank-level NSF — flag for reconciliation review.
  • Merchant request to modify terms — escalate per Escalation Path.

Daily handling

An Active PA means the account already has a working repayment schedule. Your job is to keep it in flight.

What to watch

  • Missed debit attempt — outreach within 24 hours.
  • Bank-level NSF — flag for reconciliation review.
  • Merchant request to modify terms — escalate per Escalation Path.
Approval tiers for settlement offers and the documentation each tier requires.

Tiers

  • Tier 1 (≥75% of balance) — agent-approved.
  • Tier 2 (60–74%) — supervisor approval.
  • Tier 3 (<60%) — director approval, with hardship documentation.

Tiers

  • Tier 1 (≥75% of balance) — agent-approved.
  • Tier 2 (60–74%) — supervisor approval.
  • Tier 3 (<60%) — director approval, with hardship documentation.
A textbook handle of an "I can't pay today" objection that ended with a 7-day plan.

What worked

  • Acknowledged before redirecting.
  • Offered a smaller first step instead of pushing the original amount.
  • Confirmed the plan in writing while still on the call.
Tiered escalation map across collections, compliance, and legal.

Tiers

  • Tier 1 — Team Lead: standard exceptions, terms inside policy.
  • Tier 2 — Manager: settlement outside Tier 1 limits, ADDS extensions.
  • Tier 3 — Director: legal-adjacent disputes, regulatory mentions, large settlements.

What to send

Always include the account ID, a one-line summary of what you've already tried, and the specific decision you need.

Tiers

  • Tier 1 — Team Lead: standard exceptions, terms inside policy.
  • Tier 2 — Manager: settlement outside Tier 1 limits, ADDS extensions.
  • Tier 3 — Director: legal-adjacent disputes, regulatory mentions, large settlements.

What to send

Always include the account ID, a one-line summary of what you've already tried, and the specific decision you need.
Post-judgment garnishment — when, how, and the limits by state.

Eligibility

Garnishment requires a finalized judgment plus a verified bank or income source. State limits override our internal targets.

Workflow

  • Confirm judgment is recorded.
  • Submit garnishment request to legal partner.
  • Track service of process and respond to objections.

Eligibility

Garnishment requires a finalized judgment plus a verified bank or income source. State limits override our internal targets.

Workflow

  • Confirm judgment is recorded.
  • Submit garnishment request to legal partner.
  • Track service of process and respond to objections.
Day-to-day handling of accounts in active litigation, including comms restrictions.

Comms freeze

Once an account enters active litigation, all merchant outreach must route through legal. Direct contact is prohibited.

Internal updates

  • Weekly status review with the legal partner.
  • Document every notice received and forward to legal within one business day.

Comms freeze

Once an account enters active litigation, all merchant outreach must route through legal. Direct contact is prohibited.

Internal updates

  • Weekly status review with the legal partner.
  • Document every notice received and forward to legal within one business day.
Filing for judgment — required documentation, timelines, and merchant notice.

When we file

Judgment is sought after exhaustion of Rehab 1, Rehab 2, and at least one settlement attempt — with full documentation.

Pre-filing checklist

  • Signed agreement on file.
  • Complete payment history.
  • Most recent merchant communication log.
  • Notice-of-intent letter sent and acknowledged or returned.

When we file

Judgment is sought after exhaustion of Rehab 1, Rehab 2, and at least one settlement attempt — with full documentation.

Pre-filing checklist

  • Signed agreement on file.
  • Complete payment history.
  • Most recent merchant communication log.
  • Notice-of-intent letter sent and acknowledged or returned.
Which advances qualify as non-recourse and how it affects collections handling.

Eligibility

Non-recourse advances are flagged at funding. You'll see a "non-recourse" tag on the account header.

Effect on collections

  • No personal guarantee enforcement.
  • Reconciliation requests handled differently — see Reconciliation policy.
  • Legal escalation requires senior approval.

Eligibility

Non-recourse advances are flagged at funding. You'll see a "non-recourse" tag on the account header.

Effect on collections

  • No personal guarantee enforcement.
  • Reconciliation requests handled differently — see Reconciliation policy.
  • Legal escalation requires senior approval.
When ACH runs go out, posting timing, and how to handle returned items.

Schedule

ACH files are released at 3:00 PM ET, Monday–Friday. Funds typically post within 1–2 business days.

Returns

  • R01 (insufficient funds) — auto-retry once after 3 business days.
  • R02 (account closed) — outreach required, no retry.
  • R10 (unauthorized) — escalate immediately to compliance.

Schedule

ACH files are released at 3:00 PM ET, Monday–Friday. Funds typically post within 1–2 business days.

Returns

  • R01 (insufficient funds) — auto-retry once after 3 business days.
  • R02 (account closed) — outreach required, no retry.
  • R10 (unauthorized) — escalate immediately to compliance.
How to post a manual payment, reverse a misposting, and audit the result.

Manual posting

Manual posts require a manager approval ticket and a copy of the deposit confirmation. Use the "Manual Post" template.

Reversals

Misposted payments must be reversed within the same business day. After cutoff, reversals require a reconciliation ticket.

Manual posting

Triggers that move an account from Rehab 1 into Rehab 2 or out to legal review.

Escalation triggers

  • Three consecutive no-contact attempts.
  • Promised payment broken twice in the same cycle.
  • Merchant disputes the balance with documentation.
Documentation, review steps, and turn-around for a reconciliation request.

Required docs

  • Three months of merchant processing statements.
  • Bank statements covering the same period.
  • Signed reconciliation request form.

Turn-around

Standard review is 5 business days. Communicate the timeline at the moment of request — don't promise faster.

Required docs

  • Three months of merchant processing statements.
  • Bank statements covering the same period.
  • Signed reconciliation request form.

Turn-around

Right-party contact

Open

"Hi, this is [name] from McKenzie Capital. May I speak with [merchant name]?"

If reached

Purpose

"I'm calling about your business funding agreement — there's a missed payment we'd like to resolve today before it advances."

Open

"Hi, this is [name] from McKenzie Capital. May I speak with [merchant name]?"

If reached

Purpose

When a merchant can cancel, the window, and what we must disclose.

Window

Merchants may cancel within the disclosed window from the agreement signing date. Required disclosure language is published in Compliance.

Process

  • Cancellation request must be in writing.
  • Funds (if disbursed) reversed within five business days.
  • Account closed with note: "Cancelled within window."

Window

Merchants may cancel within the disclosed window from the agreement signing date. Required disclosure language is published in Compliance.

Process

  • Cancellation request must be in writing.
  • Funds (if disbursed) reversed within five business days.
  • Account closed with note: "Cancelled within window."
Opening, mid-call, and close talk track for inbound Rehab 1 calls.

Opening

Open

"Thank you for calling McKenzie Capital, this is [name]. I'm here to help — can I start with the business name on the account?"

Mid-call

Acknowledge & redirect

"I hear you. Let's look at what we can put together today so this doesn't follow you into the next cycle."

Close

Confirm

"To recap, we have a payment of $X scheduled for [date]. You'll see a confirmation email within the hour."

How Rehab 1 fits into the collections lifecycle and the goals of the first cycle.

Where Rehab 1 sits

Rehab 1 is the first cure cycle after a missed payment. The goal is to cure the account before it advances to Rehab 2 or charge-off.

Standard cadence

  • Day 1–3: courtesy contact attempts (inbound + outbound).
  • Day 4–10: structured outreach with payment options.
  • Day 11+: review for promotion to Rehab 2.

Reminder

Always document every attempt in the account note, even if there's no response.

  • Day 11+: review for promotion to Rehab 2.

Reminder

Required notes, documentation, and tags before passing an account to Rehab 2.

Before you hand off

  • All contact attempts logged with timestamp and outcome.
  • Most recent merchant disposition tag set.
  • Latest balance reconciled against payment processor.
  • Any compliance flags or disputes attached.
Talk track for servicing an active payment arrangement on a charged-off account.

Reinforcement

Affirm

"Your arrangement is in good standing — I just want to confirm the next debit date and that your bank info hasn't changed."
Talk track for setting up a first-time payment arrangement post-charge-off.

Open

Frame

"Your account has charged off, but that doesn't mean we stop here. Let me walk you through a payment arrangement that keeps this from advancing further."

Set the terms

  • Confirm balance and any allowable settlement reduction.
  • Lock the first debit date within 14 days.
  • Send written confirmation before the call ends.

Second-stage rehab handling for accounts that didn't cure in Rehab 1.

Rehab 2 in one paragraph

Rehab 2 picks up accounts that didn't cure in the first cycle. Tone shifts from courtesy to firm — every conversation should end with either a payment, a payment plan, or a clear escalation path.

Sub-flows

  • New Default Account — first-time advance to Rehab 2.
  • PCO Account — payment-cure obligation account, requires structured plan.

Sub-flows

  • New Default Account — first-time advance to Rehab 2.
  • PCO Account — payment-cure obligation account, requires structured plan.
Talk track for accounts already on a payment-cure obligation.

Open

Reset

"You've already agreed to a cure schedule, and we're seeing missed payments against it. Let's identify what changed today."

Discovery

  • What changed in your business since the plan started?
  • Is the bank account still active for ACH?
  • Do you need to adjust the debit date inside the same month?

Open

Reset

"You've already agreed to a cure schedule, and we're seeing missed payments against it. Let's identify what changed today."

Discovery

  • What changed in your business since the plan started?
  • Is the bank account still active for ACH?
  • Do you need to adjust the debit date inside the same month?
Tone, framing, and a suggested talk track for first-time Rehab 2 accounts.

Setting the frame

Open

"Your account has advanced because the previous attempts didn't resolve it. I want to talk through your options today so this doesn't escalate further."

Two-option close

  • Cure today with a single catch-up payment.
  • Structured plan over the next 30 days.
